commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a legacy `openai-codex` context-config fallback for official OpenAI Codex-runtime routes, passes model IDs into the fallback resolver, and adds focused routing/setup tests.

PR surface: Source +119, Tests +73. Total +192 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: yes. The linked report and PR body give a source-level path where v2026.6.1 resolves the context provider to canonical `openai`, so a legacy-only `openai-codex.models[].contextTokens` entry is missed and the Codex runtime metadata supplies 272000.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can align the provider-level fallback detector with the runtime context resolver without changing the broader OpenAI/Codex routing design.

target.title: fix(codex): honor legacy context overrides after OpenAI unification
target.body:
## Summary

This PR preserves legacy Codex context-window overrides after the 2026.6.1 OpenAI/Codex provider unification.

- If `openai/gpt-*` is running through the Codex runtime and canonical `models.providers.openai` has no explicit context metadata for the selected model, the context lookup can now fall back to legacy `models.providers.openai-codex` context metadata.
- Canonical `openai` context metadata still wins when present.
- Custom OpenAI-compatible `baseUrl` configs keep using canonical `openai`; the fallback is only for official OpenAI + Codex runtime context lookup.
- Auth/runtime provider routing remains unified under `openai`; this PR only affects context-config provider selection.

Why now: 2026.5.28-era installs could configure `models.providers.openai-codex.models[].contextTokens` around 1M. After 2026.6.1 unification, the context resolver can miss that metadata and fall back to the Codex runtime default `272000`.

Out of scope: changing the default Codex cap, changing Codex projection/context-engine behavior, or reviving any durable Telegram/session-context persistence behavior from older closed work.

Review focus: the compatibility fallback in `openai-routing.ts`, the model-specific/canonical-wins guardrails, and the call sites now passing `modelId` into context-config provider resolution.

## Linked context

Closes #90448

Related #77858
Related #77861

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `openai/gpt-5.5` running through Codex can lose a legacy `models.providers.openai-codex.models[].contextTokens` override after 2026.6.1 and fall back to `272000`.
- Real environment tested: macOS local OpenClaw source checkout on this PR branch (`agent/xiaozhua/codex-context-override-migration`, commit `7add9890d6`) with the real OpenClaw resolver/setup code. The original regression was observed on a Windows OpenClaw node upgraded from official `2026.5.28` to official `2026.6.1`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node --import tsx - <<'EOF'
import { resolveContextConfigProviderForRuntime } from './src/agents/openai-routing.ts';
import { resolveEffectiveRuntimeModel } from './src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/setup.ts';
const cfg = { models: { providers: { openai: { models: [] }, 'openai-codex': { models: [{ id: 'gpt-5.5', contextTokens: 1050000, contextWindow: 400000 }] } } } };
const contextConfigProvider = resolveContextConfigProviderForRuntime({ provider: 'openai', runtimeId: 'codex', modelId: 'gpt-5.5', config: cfg });
const result = resolveEffectiveRuntimeModel({ cfg, provider: 'openai', contextConfigProvider, modelId: 'gpt-5.5', runtimeModel: { provider: 'openai', id: 'gpt-5.5', name: 'gpt-5.5', baseUrl: 'https://api.openai.com/v1', api: 'openai-responses', reasoning: true, input: ['text'], cost: { input: 0, output: 0, cacheRead: 0, cacheWrite: 0 }, contextWindow: 1050000, contextTokens: 272000, maxTokens: 128000 } });
console.log(JSON.stringify({ contextConfigProvider, ctxInfo: result.ctxInfo, effectiveContextWindow: result.effectiveModel.contextWindow }));
EOF
```

- Evidence after fix:

```json
{"contextConfigProvider":"openai-codex","ctxInfo":{"tokens":1050000,"source":"modelsConfig"},"effectiveContextWindow":1050000}
```

- Observed result after fix: a 5.28-era legacy `openai-codex` context override is honored for an official `openai/gpt-5.5` Codex-runtime route when canonical `openai` has no model context override.
- What was not tested: I did not install this PR branch onto the Windows node or mutate its production config.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The after-fix proof is a PR-branch source-level runtime setup probe plus focused tests, not a full Gateway install. This avoids touching existing OAuth/session state while still exercising the real resolver and setup code path changed by the PR.
- Before evidence: On current main before this patch, the same legacy-only config resolved to the fallback/default shape: `{"name":"cfgLegacy","contextConfigProvider":"openai","tokens":272000}`. Canonical `models.providers.openai.models[]` with the same override resolved to `1050000`, confirming the regression is the legacy provider compatibility gap.

## Tests and validation

Commands run:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openai-routing.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/setup.test.ts --testNamePattern="context"
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openai-routing.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/setup.test.ts
git diff --check
```

Results:

```text
[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 23.31s
[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 23.89s
```

Regression coverage added:

- `openai-routing.test.ts` verifies legacy `openai-codex` context metadata is used only when canonical `openai` metadata is absent.
- `openai-routing.test.ts` verifies canonical `openai` metadata wins over legacy metadata.
- `setup.test.ts` verifies the effective runtime model context window resolves to `1_050_000` for the 5.28-era legacy config shape instead of the runtime `272_000` cap.

What failed before this fix: source-level probing on current main showed legacy-only `openai-codex` context metadata produced `272000` for the unified OpenAI route.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Existing legacy Codex context overrides can again affect displayed/effective context budgets for official OpenAI Codex-runtime routes.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes, compatibility behavior changes for legacy context metadata. No config files are rewritten by this PR.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No. Auth/runtime provider routing remains canonical `openai`; this only selects the provider key used for context-window config lookup.

What is the highest-risk area?

Accidentally applying a legacy Codex context override when canonical OpenAI metadata or a custom OpenAI-compatible endpoint should be authoritative.

How is that risk mitigated?

The fallback is guarded by all of these conditions: selected provider is OpenAI, runtime is Codex, OpenAI is not configured with a custom `baseUrl`, canonical `openai` has no positive context override for the model, and legacy `openai-codex` has positive context metadata for that model. Tests cover canonical-wins behavior.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Wait for CI and ClawSweeper review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

CI/ClawSweeper are pending. I can add a full installed-Gateway proof if maintainers want it, but avoided mutating production OAuth/session state for the initial PR.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

None yet; initial submission.
target.author: ooiuuii

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
